nodes.x: Out of the depths have I cried unto thee, O Lord.
Hear my prayer, O Lord, and let my cry come unto thee.
I cried to thee, O Lord; and unto the Lord I made supplication.
But unto thee have I cried, O Lord; and in the morning shall my prayer prevent thee.
O Lord my God, I cried unto thee, and thou hast healed me.
O Lord God of my salvation, I have cried day and night before thee:
Lord, hear my voice: let thine ears be attentive to the voice of my supplications.
I said unto the Lord, Thou art my God: hear the voice of my supplications, O Lord.
Blessed be the Lord, because he hath heard the voice of my supplications.
Thou hast heard my voice: hide not thine ear at my breathing, at my cry.
Nevertheless hear thou now this word that I speak in thine ears, and in the ears of all the people;
For I said in my haste, I am cut off from before thine eyes: nevertheless thou heardest the voice of my supplications when I cried unto thee.
If thou, Lord, shouldest mark iniquities, O Lord, who shall stand?

shall fail before his majesty. Who shall be 'able to abide the glory of his brow and stand in his presence unconsumed? They alone who have hearkened to his Voice,- who have kept his commandments, and have been 'washed, and purified* and prepared through his sacred ordinances.
And behold, if the Lord shall say unto a man—Because of thine iniquities, thou shalt be accursed forever—it shall be done.
thee from iniquity, and that thou shouldst become to Him a son, and a servant, and a minister of His presence.
Then both the men, between whom the controversy is, shall stand before the Lord, before the priests and the judges, which shall be in those days;
If I sin, then thou markest me, and thou wilt not acquit me from mine iniquity.
But there is forgiveness with thee, that thou mayest be feared.
But as for thee and thy servants, I know that ye will not yet fear the Lord God.
Abide thou with me, fear not: for he that seeketh my life seeketh thy life: but with me thou shalt be in safeguard.
Thou, even thou, art to be feared: and who may stand in thy sight when once thou art angry?
Will he reprove thee for fear of thee? will he enter with thee into judgment?
Against thee, thee only, have I sinned, and done this evil in thy sight: that thou mightest be justified when thou speakest, and be clear when thou judgest.
I wait for the Lord, my soul doth wait, and in his word do I hope.
I have waited for thy salvation, O Lord.
Truly my soul waiteth upon God: from him cometh my salvation.
And now, Lord, what wait I for? my hope is in thee.
¶ And it shall be said in that day, Lo, this is our God; we have waited for him, and he will save us: this is the Lord; we have waited for him, we will be glad and rejoice in his salvation.
I waited patiently for the Lord; and he inclined unto me, and heard my cry.
My soul waiteth for the Lord more than they that watch for the morning: I say, more than they that watch for the morning.
And in the morning, then ye shall see the glory of the Lord; for that he heareth your murmurings against the Lord: and what are we, that ye murmur against us?
My voice shalt thou hear in the morning, O Lord; in the morning will I direct my prayer unto thee, and will look up.
And what I say unto one, I say unto all men; watch, therefore, for you know not at what hour your Lord doth come.
With my soul have I desired thee in the night; yea, with my spirit within me will I seek thee early: for when thy judgments are in the earth, the inhabitants of the world will learn righteousness.
Let Israel hope in the Lord: for with the Lord there is mercy, and with him is plenteous redemption.
Let Israel hope in the Lord from henceforth and for ever.
Let Israel now say, that his mercy endureth for ever.
And made Israel to pass through the midst of it: for his mercy endureth for ever:
And brought out Israel from among them: for his mercy endureth for ever:
And he shall redeem Israel from all his iniquities.
May God bless his people, redeem and save Israel, and hasten his work in its time.
And many of the children of Israel shall he turn to the Lord their God.
Redeem Israel, O God, out of all his troubles.
And he shall give Israel up because of the sins of Jeroboam, who did sin, and who made Israel to sin.
And also the Strength of Israel will not lie nor repent: for he is not a man, that he should repent.
